title: Per-Transaction MEV Classification Execution
why_now: The opportunity archive uses structural frequency estimates (e.g., arbitrage at 2-5% of transactions). Executing actual GROUP BY and pattern-matching queries against canonical_execution_traces would produce ground-truth counts for each archived class, replacing bounded estimates with empirical data.

title: Temporal Stability of Opportunity Class Prevalence
why_now: Every opportunity class in the archive has 'temporal stability: unknown' because all estimates come from single-day windows. Multi-day or multi-week sampling of the same tables would reveal whether class frequencies and competitive intensities are stable or regime-dependent — a critical input for strategy feasibility.

title: Trace-Log Correlation for Token MEV Archive Extension
why_now: The archive identifies token-denominated MEV as the primary data gap — ETH-only trace analysis misses the most economically significant MEV classes. Joining canonical_execution_traces with canonical_execution_logs (ERC-20 Transfer events) would extend the archive to cover token arbitrage and sandwich classes.
